evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Endpoints (/import, /api/query/engage) and Basic Auth with service accounts are consistent with Mixpanel conventions. However mixpanel_base_url() returns https://mixpanel.com (or https://eu.mixpanel.com) which is wrong — should be api.mixpanel.com / api-eu.mixpanel.com. This is an internal inconsistency since the prose says https://api.mixpanel.com/import. Pydantic BaseSettings import path is v1-style (in Pydantic v2 it moved to pydantic_settings), a likely freshness issue.
evidence.executability: Code blocks parse but several runtime issues: from pydantic import BaseSettings fails on modern pydantic v2 installs; mixpanel_base_url returns wrong host; the @app.on_event lifespan hooks are deprecated; test uses TestClient with @patch on http_client but mock_resp.raise_for_status is AsyncMock when httpx response.raise_for_status is sync; duplicate '$insert_id' check is a typo. No sandbox-infeasible patterns detected.
evidence.completeness: (a) credentials acquisition described, (b) pip install bash present, (c) env template present with exact names matching BaseSettings fields, (d) backend FastAPI code present, (e) no frontend (FastAPI-only stack, acceptable), (f) test command shown via pytest snippet but no explicit 'pytest' run command in a bash block. Env vars listed match those referenced.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Multiple official developer.mixpanel.com and docs.mixpanel.com URLs cited inline near relevant code/sections; also fastapi.tiangolo.com. Some github.com/mixpanel/docs links are semi-official. Placement is near code, not just at end.

rubric_notes: docs_unavailable=true; long-tail handling applied. Internal inconsistency between prose URL (api.mixpanel.com) and code (mixpanel.com host) lowers correctness; pydantic BaseSettings import is v1 pattern.
